symptoms.symptom_name: Palpitations / sensation of pounding, racing heart

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpitations are a core somatic feature of panic arousal.
Palpitations must be screened for a cardiac cause before attribution to anxiety.
Palpitations occur in hyperthyroidism, especially with weight loss.

symptoms.symptom_name: Insomnia, tremor and psychomotor agitation over 48 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute insomnia, tremor and agitation are typical of an acute anxiety state.

symptoms.symptom_name: Distress tied to a single recent event (awaiting results), not pervasive chronic worry

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A single acute stressor points to a situational panic reaction rather than generalised anxiety disorder.
GAD requires pervasive, longstanding worry across multiple domains, which is absent here.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of ischaemic chest pain (no central/crushing pain, no radiation, no exertional trigger)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of ischaemic chest pain features lowers the probability of ACS, though it does not exclude it entirely in telehealth.
Palpitations without ischaemic pain fit an anxiety-driven tachycardia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional weight loss and fatigue (prompted the workup)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Constitutional weight loss and fatigue are the red-flag features that warranted the completed investigation panel.
Weight loss with palpitations can indicate hyperthyroidism — thyroid function testing is reasonable.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia HR 110, BP 145/90, RR 22, SpO2 99%, afebrile (clinician-measured on home visit)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild hypertension and tachycardia with normal saturations and temperature fit acute sympathetic arousal. Sealed clinician measurement — the AI Doctor infers arousal from reported palpitations and distress, not a measured vital sign.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal completed blood panel (CBC, CMP, tumour markers within reference range)

investigation_details.investigation_name: CBC, comprehensive metabolic panel and targeted tumour markers
investigation_details.expected_result: All parameters within established reference ranges / no significant pathological abnormality
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without the completed workup, the constitutional symptoms (weight loss, fatigue) remain unexplained.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal panel including tumour markers and inflammatory indices argues against the feared malignancy. Sealed result — the AI Doctor cannot access or deliver it in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: No suicidal ideation, intent or plan

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative suicidality screen supports safe management in the community with routine follow-up.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Palpitations, insomnia and tremor tied to a single stressor, without ischaemic chest pain, form a coherent acute anxiety picture once organic mimics are screened.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude an arrhythmic or ischaemic cause of the palpitations before attributing them to anxiety — cannot be performed via telehealth chat.
Palpitations with unintentional weight loss warrant screening for thyrotoxicosis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Review of the already-completed CBC / CMP / tumour-marker panel
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The results exist and should be obtained and discussed by the GP with the patient — this addresses the weight-loss/fatigue workup and the health-anxiety fear.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Repeat or additional tumour-marker / imaging testing driven by health anxiety
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Over-investigation in response to anxiety reinforces the health-anxiety loop and is not clinically indicated once the appropriate panel is normal.
